topic: LangGraph vs CrewAI vs AutoGen 2026: Which AI Agent Framework Should You Use?

key_points: Covers role-based (CrewAI) vs graph-based (LangGraph) vs conversational (AutoGen) architectures
Hands-on code examples for each framework
Recommends CrewAI for quick prototyping, LangGraph for production stateful systems
Practical 2026 guide with real-world use case mapping
Notes AutoGen's deprecation and the AG2 fork situation
LangGraph rated best for mission-critical production deployments
Side-by-side feature table across all three frameworks
Covers learning curve: CrewAI (easiest) > AutoGen (medium) > LangGraph (steepest)
Performance benchmarks showing CrewAI 48% faster than AutoGen on structured tasks
Details LangGraph's time-travel debugging and checkpointing features
Covers token efficiency differences: AutoGen uses 20+ LLM calls per task
Broader roundup placing these three in top 10 agent frameworks
Covers Microsoft Agent Framework as the AutoGen successor
Adoption stats and GitHub star comparisons

stats.fact: LangGraph has 29,500 GitHub stars and is used in production by Klarna, Replit, and Elastic
AutoGen (Microsoft) has ~28,400 GitHub stars as of January 2026
CrewAI has ~15,200 GitHub stars (Jan 2026) but leads with ~1.3 million monthly PyPI installs vs AG2's 100,000
CrewAI is 48% faster and uses 34% fewer tokens than AutoGen on structured tasks
CrewAI enables 40% faster time-to-production vs LangGraph for standard business workflows
Microsoft placed AutoGen in maintenance mode in October 2025, merging it into the Microsoft Agent Framework (public preview Oct 2025, GA target Q1 2026)
AutoGen averaged 450,000 downloads/month in late 2025; CrewAI averaged 280,000 downloads/month
AutoGen's original creators forked it into AG2 (November 2024) after leaving Microsoft, creating a community-maintained alternative

angles: AutoGen is effectively deprecated by Microsoft — developers now must choose between AG2 (community fork) or Microsoft Agent Framework, making the landscape messier than ever
LangGraph is the clear production winner for stateful, complex pipelines but has the steepest learning curve — when is that complexity actually worth it?
CrewAI's role-based model maps naturally to how product teams already think, making it the default starting point for non-ML engineers
The cost angle: AutoGen's conversational architecture uses 20+ LLM calls per task, making it significantly more expensive at scale
Framework choice locks you in differently — CrewAI abstracts away control, LangGraph gives you full control but more boilerplate
For 2026: the rise of MCP (Model Context Protocol) and how each framework integrates with standardized tool ecosystems
outline: H2: TL;DR — Which Framework Should You Use in 2026?
H2: The State of AI Agent Frameworks in 2026 (What Changed)
H2: LangGraph — Graph-Based Orchestration for Production
H2: CrewAI — Role-Based Simplicity for Fast Delivery
H2: AutoGen / AG2 — Conversational Agents and the Microsoft Split
H2: Head-to-Head Comparison: Performance, Cost, and Developer Experience
H2: Use Case Decision Guide — Which Framework Fits Your Project?
H2: Verdict: LangGraph vs CrewAI vs AutoGen in 2026
